headline: Tennessee Breaks Ground on Revolutionary Nuclear Reactor
meta_description: Kairos Power begins construction of Hermes 2, an advanced molten salt reactor designed to be safer and power data centers with clean energy.
summary_short: A California nuclear firm has broken ground on America's first advanced reactor plant in Oak Ridge, Tennessee, set to come online in 2030.
body:
Kairos Power has begun construction on Hermes 2, the nation's first advanced nuclear reactor plant, in Oak Ridge, Tennessee. The facility represents a significant milestone in nuclear innovation—one that comes at a moment when the U.S. nuclear fleet is aging and energy demands are surging.

The molten salt reactor will operate differently from traditional nuclear plants. Rather than using pressurized water as a coolant, Hermes 2 will use molten salt that functions at ambient pressures. This fundamental design change removes a major cause of reactor accidents: the catastrophic explosions that occur when high pressure suddenly drops to low pressure, as happened during the 2011 Fukushima disaster.

The reactor will also use TRISO fuel—uranium kernels the size of poppy seeds encased in ceramic and graphite layers. Chinese demonstrations in the 2010s proved TRISO's resilience: the shells showed no damage even at temperatures of 1,620°C, far exceeding any realistic emergency scenario.

Google has signed an agreement for 500 megawatts from multiple advanced reactors, with Hermes 2 delivering the first 50 megawatts to power its Tennessee data centers. The facility is expected to come online in 2030, marking a turning point in how America powers its digital future with clean, reliable energy.
